title: EIP-7748: State conversion to Verkle Tree
description: Proposes a procedure to convert, on each block, a fixed number of key-values from the existing Merkle Patricia Tree (MPT) to the Verkle Tree (VKT). The conversion runs before transaction execution and processes storage slots and account data incrementally, bounded by a configurable stride parameter, until all state has been migrated.
author: Guillaume Ballet (@gballet), Ignacio Hagopian (@jsign), Gajinder Singh (@g11tech), Ansgar Dietrichs (@adietrichs), Gottfried Herold (@GottfriedHerold), Jamie Lokier (@jlokier), Tanishq Jasoria (@tanishqjasoria), Parithosh Jayanthi (@parithosh), Gabriel Rocheleau (@gabrocheleau), Karim Taam (@matkt)
laymanDescription: Gradually moves all of Ethereum's stored account and contract data from the old storage format (Merkle Patricia Tree) to a new, more efficient one (Verkle Tree). Each block converts a small batch so the migration completes over time without disrupting normal network operation.

endUsers.description: No direct impact during or after conversion. Transaction processing continues normally throughout the migration period.

appDevs.description: No changes to smart contract interfaces or behavior. State access semantics remain identical during and after conversion.

walletDevs.description: No wallet-facing changes. State proofs may change format after full migration to Verkle Trees, but not during conversion.

toolingInfra.description: Block explorers and node monitoring tools may need to track conversion progress and handle the dual-tree state during the migration period.

layer2s.description: No direct impact. Layer 2 protocols relying on state proofs will eventually benefit from smaller Verkle proofs after conversion completes.

stakersNodes.description: Node operators experience additional per-block processing load during conversion. Nodes must have preimage data available for the migration.

elClients.description: Significant implementation work: clients must add incremental trie iteration, conversion state tracking, stride-bounded migration logic, and preimage resolution.

clClients.description: No direct implementation required. Consensus clients are unaffected since state conversion is purely an execution layer concern.

benefits: Enables full migration to Verkle Trees, unlocking smaller stateless proofs for the network.
Incremental per-block conversion avoids a disruptive one-time state migration event.
Allows eventual removal of the Overlay Tree abstraction introduced by EIP-7612.
Conversion before transaction execution simplifies handling of stale data scenarios.
tradeoffs: Adds extra per-block processing load on execution clients for the duration of the conversion.
Requires all nodes to have MPT preimage data available, adding a prerequisite dependency.
Code chunking in a single step can temporarily exceed the stride limit by up to ~793 units.

scaleL1.description: Completing the Verkle Tree migration enables stateless clients and smaller state proofs, directly supporting L1 scalability.
